stints.stage: Group Stage (in progress) - already qualified for Round of 32
stints.notes: Third consecutive World Cup. Captain. Scored a brace vs Senegal (overtook Olivier Giroud as France's all-time top scorer in that match, on his 99th cap) and a brace vs Iraq (reaching 100 caps for France in that match - youngest player ever to reach 100 caps for France at 27). Now has 16 World Cup goals across 3 tournaments, tied for 2nd-most all-time in World Cup history behind Messi (18).

Won the Golden Boot (8 goals) and Silver Ball. Scored a hat-trick in the final vs Argentina (3-3 after extra time, lost 2-4 on penalties) - first World Cup final hat-trick since Geoff Hurst in 1966. Reached 12 World Cup goals across 2018+2022, tied with Pele for 6th all-time at the time.
Won the World Cup at age 19, his tournament debut. Scored in the final vs Croatia (4-2 win) - first teenager to score in a World Cup final since Pele in 1958. Won World Cup Best Young Player award.

career_milestones: France's all-time top goalscorer (60 goals, surpassing Olivier Giroud during the 2026 World Cup)
Youngest player to reach 100 caps for France (age 27)
Top scorer in World Cup final matches all-time (4 goals across 2018 and 2022 finals)
16 career World Cup goals across 3 tournaments (2018, 2022, 2026 to date), tied for 2nd-most all-time behind Messi

club_career.notes: Top scorer in La Liga again this season (Pichichi Trophy 2026). 31 total appearances = 29 starts + 2 sub appearances, per Real Madrid's official site breakdown.

First Real Madrid season after joining on a free transfer in June 2024. Scored 43 goals across all competitions (31 in La Liga), winning the La Liga top scorer title (Pichichi) in his debut season. Appearance/starts breakdown not yet found - needs manual lookup.

Final PSG season before leaving for Real Madrid. PSG won Ligue 1 (their 3rd consecutive title), Mbappe won Ligue 1 Player of the Year for the 5th consecutive season. Full stat breakdown not yet found.

Top scorer for the 5th consecutive Ligue 1 season as PSG won their record 11th Ligue 1 title. Became PSG's all-time leading scorer this season (passed 201 goals for the club in March 2023). Appearance/starts breakdown not yet found.
First player to finish as both Ligue 1 top scorer and top assist provider in the same season. Top scorer for the 4th consecutive season. Appearance/starts breakdown not yet found.
Top scorer for the 3rd consecutive Ligue 1 season. One goal, one assist in the Coupe de France final win over Monaco. Appearance/starts breakdown not yet found.

Joint-top scorer in Ligue 1 with 18 goals (awarded the title outright on goals-per-game ratio over Monaco's Ben Yedder). Season cut short by COVID-19; PSG crowned champions when season ended early. Reached the Champions League final, lost 0-1 to Bayern Munich. Appearance/starts breakdown not yet found.
Ligue 1 Player of the Year, top scorer with 33 goals - his highest Ligue 1 goal tally in a single season. Appearance/starts breakdown not yet found.
First PSG season, signed from Monaco on loan in August 2017 (mandatory purchase option of 180 million euros). PSG won Ligue 1. Stats not yet found - needs manual lookup.

Career-breakthrough season. 26 goals from 44 matches across all competitions. Helped Monaco win their first Ligue 1 title in 17 years. Reached Champions League semi-finals (scored twice in the quarter-final 2nd leg vs Borussia Dortmund). Starts not broken out separately from this prose source.

Senior debut on 2 December 2015 at age 16 - youngest first-team player in Monaco's history at the time (since surpassed). Scored only 1 goal with limited playing time. Full appearance count not yet found.

data_quality_notes: Built using the Haaland-established schema and field set: appearances + starts both stored independently where known, optional cards, needs_breakdown flag for unresolved gaps, advanced stat tier reserved for seasons with a detailed stat feed (none populated yet for Mbappe - none of the structured per-season tables used for Haaland have been supplied for Mbappe yet).

SIGNIFICANT GAP: most of Mbappe's PSG career (2017-18 through 2023-24, 7 seasons) currently only has total goals from prose sources, with appearances/starts/assists largely unresolved - flagged needs_breakdown across the board. This is a much bigger gap than Haaland's file had, since no structured stat table has been supplied for Mbappe's PSG years yet. If/when a FotMob-style per-season table is available for these PSG seasons (the same format used for Haaland's Man City/Dortmund/Salzburg seasons), it should be slotted in to replace these placeholders.

Real Madrid 2025-26 season is the one exception with a fuller breakdown (29 starts + 2 sub = 31 appearances), sourced from Real Madrid's official site, which presented it in a season-stats format close to (but not identical to) the structured per-season tables used for Haaland.
Real Madrid 2024-25 (debut season) also needs_breakdown - only total goals (31 La Liga / 43 all competitions) confirmed so far.

National team: caps_total (100) and goals_total (60) are current as of his 100th-cap match (France 3-0 Iraq, 2026-06-22) - these will need updating again once the 2026 World Cup concludes, same as Haaland's Norway totals.

Monaco 2016-17 appearances (44) is an all-competitions total from prose, not league-only and not split into starts - same caveat pattern as Haaland's Molde 2018 entry before it was corrected by a structured table.
Sources: Wikipedia (player page + international goals list), FotMob, Sofascore, FBref, Real Madrid official site, Transfermarkt, Britannica, ESPN, FIFA.com, UEFA.com (June 2026).
